1.14: Firms may act if the person or immediate family is the seller, provided a separate partner (unrelated to the seller) acts for the lender.
Must ensure no conflict of interest and adherence to Law Society guidelines.
1.15: The firm may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or an immediate family member is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's conveyancer name and address is not required.
3.1.4: Contact point if seller is unrepresented: Conveyancing@habito.com or Hey Habito LTD, Mr A Thomson, 14 Gower's Walk, London E1 8PY
3.1.5: No other documents are acceptable for verifying identity.

4.5b: Certificates of Title must be sent to Conveyancing@habito.com.
4.1: Physical valuation reports are included in the offer pack.
4.3: Incorrect valuation assumptions must be reported to the contact details in 1.11.
5.1.1: The seller or remortgage applicant must have been the registered owner for at least 6 months prior to the application date.
5.2.1: Notify the lender if the seller is not the registered proprietor via 1.11 contact details.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the firm is CoPSO registered, has adequate PI insurance, and complies with the Search Code.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted if it protects the lender and the conveyancer is satisfied the title is good and marketable.
5.5.3b: Original or copy planning permission is not required.
5.5.3c: Original or copy building regulation consents are not required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required.
5.7.1a: Lending on flying freeholds is permitted if the flying area is no more than 20% of the total floor area.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are generally not accepted; contact lender via 1.11 for specific cases.
5.8.5: For specific cross-over leasehold/freehold arrangements, a first charge is required over both the freehold and leasehold titles.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absentee or insolvent landlords is accepted if it renders the title good and marketable.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term must be 50 years at the end of the mortgage term.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is not accepted.
5.14.12: Ground rent or service charge receipts do not need to be sent.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service does not need to be sent.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates do not need to be sent.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms do not need to be sent.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association do not need to be sent.
5.17.5a: Powers of attorney do not need to be sent.
5.17.5b: Statutory declarations of non-revocation for powers of attorney do not need to be sent.
5.19.1: Lending on affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership is not permitted.
5.20.1: Conveyancers must report leases of roof space for solar panels that do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
5.20.3: Solar PV roof space leases are accepted if UK Finance minimum requirements are met and no separate lease agreement exists.
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed.
6.4.4: Incentives must be reported using the Disclosure of Incentives Form.
6.5.1: Residential properties require vacant possession on completion.
BTL properties allow ASTs if: tenant is an individual, property is tenant's only/principal home, tenancy is dated on or after 28/02/1997, and cannot become an assured tenancy.
6.6.3: Counterpart or certified copy tenancy agreements do not need to be sent.
6.6.4: Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) are not accepted.
6.7.1: Accepted warranty schemes: NHBC, Zurich (requires Armour Risk Management confirmation), LABC, Build Zone, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plan, Checkmate Castle, Advantage Warranties, Global Home Warranties, The Q Policy, Build Assure, International Construction Warranties, Protek.
6.7.2: New home warranty documentation should be retained by the borrower.
6.7.4: If no warranty is present, a Professional Consultant's Certificate in UK Finance format is required from a consultant with adequate PI insurance.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificates do not need to be sent.
7.3: Occupiers Deed required for all occupants aged 17 or over.
8.1: Conveyancers may advise third parties if no conflict of interest exists and the document remains legally robust.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies do not need to be sent to the lender.
9.2: Indemnity insurance limit must be the higher of the purchase price or valuation.
10.2b: Notice period for advance: 3 working days (72 hours).
Specify advance receipt date and actual completion date on the Certificate of Title.
10.7: Delayed completion: Return funds within 48 hours of receipt.
Electronic return to original bank account using Application Number or Borrower Surname as reference.
10.10: Funds can be held for a maximum of 48 hours before return.
10.11: No interest is charged if funds are returned within 48 hours.
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is not required.
